item.title: Nitrile and latex gloves may cause overestimation of microplastics, U-M study reveals
item.description: Nitrile and latex gloves that scientists wear while they are measuring microplastics may lead to a potential overestimation of the tiny pollutants, according to a University of Michigan study.

item.title: CSS is DOOMed!
item.description: Every wall, floor, barrel, and imp is a div — DOOM rendered entirely in CSS. Using 3D transforms, CSS math functions, @property, clip-path, anchor positioning, and SVG filters to build a fully playable 3D first-person shooter in the browser without Canvas or WebGL.

item.title: How I accidentally made the fastest C# CSV parser
item.description: (with certain files under certain conditions)

item.title: Mockup 147 — The Editorial Engine
item.description: Editorial pretext demo: animated orbs, live multi-column text flow, and zero DOM measurements.

item.title: I Beat the Benchmark and Still Failed
item.description: I recently learned a harsh lesson about building startups. You can build a product that beats your competitors in both quality and addictiveness, and still fail miserably if you don't know how to distribute it.

item.title: When All You Can Do Is All or Nothing, Do Nothing – CSS Wizardry
item.description: If your design system can only apply `loading=lazy` or `fetchpriority=high` blindly, it may be safer not to apply them at all.

item.title: I accidentally spammed a year of calendar invites
item.description: A calendar sync went sideways, people across my past and future started getting meeting invites from me again, and I spent the afternoon replacing the whole setup with a safer read-only tool.
item.author: (Matt Farrugia)

item.title: Building an E2E Encrypted Chat Application with LanceDB and libsodium
item.description: Justin Miller's Blog

item.title: How I Built a Public RAG App for Chicago Budget Documents
item.description: A practical build log of creating a public-facing RAG app over large municipal budget PDFs, with source citations, embedded document viewing, quality tuning, and production deployment.

item.title: How the (Em)ighty Have Fallen—
item.description: A journey of the em dash, my obsession with it growing up, my deep appreciation, and the modern constraints upon its usage.

item.title: Your Data Never Dies
item.description: The purpose fades. Your data doesn't.

item.title: Everyone is building a software factory - exe.dev blog
item.description: Is exe.dev the compute primitive you've been waiting for?
item.author: (Philip Zeyliger)

item.title: Tell the Model What to Do, Not What to Avoid
item.description: Blind A/B tests on Go code generation showed positive framing in agent instructions consistently outperforms prohibition language. I built a linter to enforce it.

item.title: The Revenge of the Data Scientist – Hamel’s Blog - Hamel Husain
item.description: Is Data Science in decline?

item.title: The Porsche Diffusion
item.description: The Porsche Diffusion

item.title: Sycophantic behavior in AI affects us all, say researchers
item.description: : Sycophantic bots coach users into selfish, antisocial behavior, say researchers, and they love it

item.title: I Decompiled the White House's New App
item.description: The official White House Android app has a cookie/paywall bypass injector, tracks your GPS every 4.5 minutes, and loads JavaScript from some guy's GitHub Pages.

item.title: Cancer
item.description: I’ve taken agency in the treatment of my bone cancer (osteosarcoma in the T5 vertebrae of the upper spine). After I’ve ran out of standard of care treatment options and there were no trials available…

item.title: CERN Uses Tiny AI Models Burned into Silicon for Real-Time LHC Data Filtering
item.description: CERN has developed ultra-small AI models embedded directly into custom chips to filter massive data streams from the Large Hadron Collider in real time, addressing the enormous data challenge of the world’s most powerful particle accelerator.

item.title: GitHub - J-x-Z/cocoa-way: Native macOS Wayland Compositor written in Rust using Smithay. Experience seamless Linux app streaming on macOS without XQuartz.
item.description: Native macOS Wayland Compositor written in Rust using Smithay. Experience seamless Linux app streaming on macOS without XQuartz. - J-x-Z/cocoa-way

item.title: How I Built an Open-World Engine for the N64
item.description:
I built a custom open-world engine for the N64. In this video, I break down how I achieved a seamless, massive world with zero loading screens on N64 hardware.
You can download it here https://github.com/lambertjamesd/n64brew2025/releases
Here is the team that helped me work on this.
Pyroxene: https://x.com/pyrox3ne Caitlin G Cooke: https://cgcooke.itch.io/ terzdesign Kælin: https://betadynast.bandcamp.com/
I will be using these same techniques in my ongoing project. If you want to support this work and follow the development journey, you can find me on
Patreon: patreon.com/JamesLambert
item.author: (James Lambert)

item.title: AMD's Ryzen 9 9950X3D2 Dual Edition crams 208MB of cache into a single chip
item.description: Both of the chip's CPU dies will include 64MB of extra cache stacked beneath.

item.title: National Grid: Live
item.description: Shows the live status of Great Britain’s electric power transmission network
